mechanism_narrative: PAYEMS trend growth reflects corporate hiring momentum that validates economic expansion and supports earnings expectations, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS trend fails to positively predict 20-day returns with Rank IC below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5%, restrictive monetary policy elevates borrowing costs and discount rates sufficiently to depress equity valuations over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55 percent of triggered instances when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5% over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: High retail sales (RSAFS) combined with low unemployment (UNRATE) indicates demand-pull economic strength that drives corporate revenue growth and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction between RSAFS and UNRATE fails to achieve a Rank IC above 0.03 for predicting 20-day returns over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During high inflation regimes, an elevated CPIAUCSL level combined with restrictive FEDFUNDS above 4% sustains high discount rates and compresses equity risk premiums, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day returns are positive in more than 50 percent of observations when CPIAUCSL exceeds 300 and FEDFUNDS exceeds 4% during walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) below 103.5 signals weakening manufacturing momentum and inventory drawdowns that precede earnings downgrades, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO below 103.5 does not predict negative 60-day returns with a hit rate below 55 percent or positive Rank IC over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) above 1400 thousand units reflect strong construction sector momentum that transmits into broad economic activity and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: HOUST above 1400 coincides with positive 5-day returns in fewer than 60 percent of instances over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Low unemployment (UNRATE) combined with high federal funds rates (FEDFUNDS) indicates restrictive policy in a tight labor market that squeezes corporate margins and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction between UNRATE and FEDFUNDS fails to achieve a Rank IC below -0.03 for predicting 20-day returns over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP (GDPC1) expansion indicates aggregate demand strength that translates into broad corporate revenue growth and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth fails to positively predict 60-day returns with Rank IC below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.
